title: potamogeton × lanceolatus subsp. rivularis
intro: Potamogeton × lanceolatus subsp. rivularis is a hybrid aquatic plant species, resulting from the cross between the American fan-leaved pondweed (Potamogeton lanceolatus) and the riverweed (Potamogeton rivularis). It has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Potamogeton × lanceolatus subsp. rivularis are typically made from the dried leaves and stems, which can be decocted in water or used in infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
